content.did_you_know: Amazon Brazil operations report that proactive safety reporting reduces workplace incidents by up to 40%, making employee engagement the strongest predictor of injury prevention.
content.success_stories:
# Safety Success Story

**Site GRU2** achieved zero lost-time incidents this week by implementing additional guardrails at the conveyor belt transfer point, addressing the root cause of previous pinch-point hazards.
content.hot_flag: Forklift Collision Risk: 3 incidents reported this week in warehouse operations. Implement mandatory refresher training and increase pedestrian zone markings immediately.
content.ev_hl_0: Zero serious incidents recorded this week maintains strong safety culture across Brazil Operations.
content.ev_hl_1: Recordable Incident Rate of 0.58 YTD remains within acceptable performance thresholds for the section.
content.ev_hl_2: Lost Time Incident Rate at 0.53 YTD demonstrates effective incident management and recovery protocols.
content.ev_ll_0: First aid rate of 11.40 YTD indicates elevated minor injury frequency requiring root cause analysis.
content.ev_ll_1: Lost time incident rate of 0.53 YTD suggests continued workplace hazards impacting workforce availability.
content.ev_ll_2: Zero incidents this week may mask underlying hazard exposure requiring deeper investigation and trend monitoring.
content.bar_hl_0: Dragonfly Closure Rate achieved 77.9% YTD, demonstrating strong barrier issue resolution performance.
content.bar_hl_1: Inspection OTC Week metric at 61.4% shows significant on-time completion improvement this week.
content.bar_hl_2: Near Miss reporting at 21.3 weekly indicates active hazard identification and proactive safety culture.
content.bar_ll_0: Dragonfly Rate spiked to 215.5 this week, representing a concerning 295% increase from YTD average of 54.5.
content.bar_ll_1: Inspection OTC YTD at 26.1% falls significantly below weekly performance, suggesting inconsistent compliance trends.
content.bar_ll_2: Near Miss YTD at 13.0 underperforms weekly rate of 21.3, indicating declining proactive hazard reporting trend.
